name: Missed-Call Instant Text-Back

parameters.content:
## Missed-Call Instant Text-Back

### How it works

This workflow responds immediately when a missed-call webhook is received from a phone provider. It normalizes the call payload, verifies that the event is actually a missed call, checks business-hour context, drafts a text-back message, runs it through a compliance approval workflow, sends the SMS through Twilio, and notifies the firm by email about the result.

### Setup steps

- Configure the Missed Call Webhook URL in Twilio, CallRail, or the relevant call tracking provider so missed-call events are sent to this workflow.
- Set the required n8n Variables for firm timezone, business hours, firm details, and any message text fields used by the code nodes.
- Connect Twilio credentials for the Send Text Message node and verify the sender number is SMS-enabled.
- Configure email credentials and recipient settings for the Notify Firm by Email node.
- Select and configure the referenced compliance-check sub-workflow used by the Compliance Check — Missed Call Text node.

### Customization

Adjust the business-hours logic, firm variables, and text-back template to match the firm's operating schedule and preferred messaging. The compliance sub-workflow can also be modified to enforce opt-out rules, content restrictions, or jurisdiction-specific requirements.

## Receive missed-call event

Entry cluster that receives the webhook payload, normalizes Twilio or CallRail call details, and determines whether the event qualifies as a valid missed call.

## Prepare text-back content

Nearby branch cluster that either skips non-missed-call events or, for valid missed calls, checks after-hours rules and composes the response text using firm configuration variables.

## Run compliance approval

Compliance gate cluster that sends the composed message to a sub-workflow, evaluates whether the text is approved to send, and stops the flow when the contact has opted out or fails approval.

## Send and notify firm

Final delivery cluster that sends the approved text message through Twilio, checks the delivery outcome, and emails the firm with the result.

nodes.name: When Call Missed Webhook
nodes.notes:
Receives the incoming call event from your phone system.

This URL is the address your phone system will notify whenever a call comes in. Copy the Production URL from this node (visible after you activate the workflow) and paste it into Twilio or CallRail.

Twilio setup: Twilio Console → Phone Numbers → your number → Voice & Fax → set StatusCallback URL to this address, StatusCallbackMethod POST. Enable status events: no-answer, busy, failed, canceled.

CallRail setup: CallRail → Settings → Notifications → Webhooks → paste this URL → select 'Missed call' events.

Both providers are supported — the next step (Read Call Details) automatically detects which one sent the event.

nodes.name: Normalize Call Details
nodes.notes:
Reads the call event and extracts the details the rest of the workflow needs: caller's phone number, caller's name, your firm's number, and whether this is actually a missed call.

This step works with both Twilio and CallRail — it detects which provider sent the event and pulls the right fields from each.

Key detail: Twilio sends a notification on every status change, including when calls are answered successfully. This step adds a flag (is_valid_missed) so the next step can skip answered calls and only act on genuine missed calls: no-answer, busy, failed, or canceled.

Output available to all later steps: caller_number, caller_name, firm_number, call_status, source, is_valid_missed, timestamp.

nodes.name: Check If Call Missed
nodes.notes:
Decides whether to continue or stop — only genuine missed calls get a text-back.

Twilio fires a notification on every call, including calls that were answered. Without this check, a client who reached you successfully would also receive an automated 'we missed your call' text — which would be confusing and unprofessional.

Yes (real missed call) → proceed to check the time and send the text.
No (call was answered, or no caller number) → stop here, nothing sent.

nodes.name: No Action - Not Missed Call
nodes.notes: Safely ends the workflow for calls that were answered or have no caller number. No text is sent, no email is sent. This endpoint is visible in the execution history so you can confirm the filter is working correctly.

nodes.name: Check After Hours Status
nodes.notes:
Checks what time the missed call came in so the text message can say the right thing.

A caller who missed you at 2 PM on a Tuesday gets: 'We missed your call and want to help.'
A caller who missed you at 9 PM or on a Saturday gets: 'We're currently closed but we want to help.'

The text is always sent regardless of time — this step only changes the wording.

Reads from n8n Variables (project → Variables tab):
  FIRM_TIMEZONE — your state's timezone (e.g. 'America/Chicago', 'America/Los_Angeles')
  FIRM_BUSINESS_HOURS_START / FIRM_BUSINESS_HOURS_END — office hours in 24-hour format (9 = 9 AM, 17 = 5 PM)
  FIRM_WORK_DAYS — comma-separated weekday numbers: 1,2,3,4,5 = Monday–Friday (0=Sunday, 6=Saturday)

nodes.name: Build Text-Back Message
nodes.notes:
Writes the text message the caller will receive and attaches your firm's contact details for the email alert.

The message is personalised with the caller's first name when it is available. It always ends with 'Reply STOP to opt out' — this is required by TCPA law for any automated text messages.

Business hours example: 'Hi Jane, this is Smith Law Firm. We missed your call and want to help. Book a free consult: [link] — Reply STOP to opt out.'
After hours example: 'Hi Jane, this is Smith Law Firm. We're currently closed but we want to help. Book a free consult: [link] — Reply STOP to opt out.'

Reads from n8n Variables (project → Variables tab):
  FIRM_NAME — your law firm's name as it should appear in texts
  FIRM_BOOKING_URL — your scheduling link (Calendly, Acuity, your website's contact page, etc.)
  FIRM_EMAIL — the email address where missed-call alerts should be sent
  FIRM_TWILIO_NUMBER — the phone number you registered in Twilio (must start with +1 for US numbers)

nodes.name: Send SMS via Twilio
nodes.notes:
Sends the text-back to the caller.

Recipient and message come from the compliance guardrail — opt-out has already been checked and the TCPA disclaimer verified before this node runs.

Credential: project → Credentials tab → New → search Twilio → enter Account SID and Auth Token → save as 'Twilio account'.

If Twilio returns an error, the workflow continues to the firm notification email.

nodes.name: Verify Text Delivery
nodes.notes:
Checks whether Twilio accepted the text before emailing the firm.

When Twilio successfully queues a text, it returns a confirmation ID (called a 'sid'). If the text failed — for example, the caller's number was invalid or your Twilio credentials are wrong — there is no confirmation ID.

Yes (text was accepted) → email the firm: 'Text-back sent ✓'
No (text failed) → email the firm: 'Text failed — please call this person back manually'

Either way, the firm always gets an email so no missed call falls through the cracks.

nodes.name: Email Firm Notification
nodes.notes:
Emails your firm a summary of every missed call — so nothing slips through even if the text fails.

The email tells you: who called, their number, what time they called, what text was sent (or that it failed), and a link to your booking page.

Credential to wire: In n8n go to project → Credentials tab → New, search for SMTP, and enter your email settings. This works with Gmail, Outlook 365, Zoho Mail, or any email provider that supports SMTP.

  Gmail: use App Passwords (Settings → Security → App Passwords) — not your regular password
  Outlook 365: use your email address and password; enable SMTP AUTH in the Microsoft 365 admin
  Zoho / other providers: check your provider's SMTP settings page for host, port, and credentials

Both the 'from' address and the 'to' address are set to FIRM_EMAIL (the address in Compose Text-Back) so the firm emails itself. You can change the 'to' address in this node if alerts should go to a different inbox.

The subject line adds '— Text failed' when the text did not go through, so you can spot failures at a glance in your inbox.

parameters.subject: =Missed Call: {{ $('Build Text-Back Message').item.json.caller_name }} ({{ $('Build Text-Back Message').item.json.caller_number }}){{ $json.sid ? '' : ' — Text failed' }}

nodes.name: Run Compliance Workflow
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the text-back.

Checks whether the caller has opted out. If not, confirms the TCPA opt-out instruction is present in the message and logs the send attempt to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Returns: { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

nodes.name: Check Approval to Send SMS
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved: true) → sends the text-back to the caller.
No (approved: false) → caller is on the opt-out list; message suppressed. The guardrail has already logged the suppression to your compliance sheet.

nodes.name: No Action - Contact Opted Out
nodes.notes: Caller is on the opt-out list — no text-back sent. The Bar-Compliance Guardrail has logged the suppression to your audit sheet. No further action needed.

description: Instantly texts back any caller who hangs up without reaching someone, then emails the firm the caller's details. Replaces Ruby Receptionist / Gabbyville ($300–500/mo). Trigger: Twilio StatusCallback or CallRail webhook.
